macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indexes Close Lower on February 27
items.summary: S&P 500 fell 0.46% to 6,877.36, Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.92% to 22,668.21, and Dow Jones declined 1.05% to 48,977.18 amid market volatility and tech sector pressures.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Beat Expectations but Stock Drops 5%
items.summary: Nvidia reported near-perfect earnings, yet shares fell 5%, dragging down the broader market including S&P 500 (-0.5%) and Nasdaq (-1%). Concerns over upcoming Big Tech earnings from Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft contributed to the selloff.

items.title: ISM Manufacturing Index Returns to Expansion at 52.6
items.summary: The ISM Manufacturing Index rose to 52.6 from 47.9, marking expansion for the first time in 10 months, driven by gains in new orders, production, and backlogs amid increased capital spending.

items.title: Dell Reports Record Revenue on AI Server Demand
items.summary: Dell announced record revenue with AI-related backlog growing to $22 billion, leading shares to jump over 12% after hours and up 8.2%. Netflix shares rose 8% after walking away from a deal.

items.title: Dow Jones Transportation Average Up 13% YTD
items.summary: The Dow Jones Transportation Average gained 13% year-to-date versus 0.36% for S&P 500, signaling positive economic momentum in trucking, airlines, and shipping alongside strong ISM data.

items.title: US Indexes Show Mixed Technical Outlook with Key Levels
items.summary: Dow Jones holds in 48,600-49,600 range with resistance at 2H 50-period MA; S&P 500 mixed below 6,890 MAs, downside below 6,832; Nasdaq faces resistance at 25,000 and support at 24,744.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Declines Amid US Inflation Data and Tether Freeze
items.summary: Crypto markets dropped with BTC down 1.69% to $65,934 and ETH falling 3.78% to $1,931.69, pressured by US inflation data delaying Fed rate cuts and Tether freezing $4.2B USDT. LayerZero (ZRO) outperformed with +4.02% gains due to adoption.

items.title: Bitcoin Briefly Surpasses $66,000 USDT
items.summary: Bitcoin crossed the 66,000 USDT mark early on Feb 28, trading at 66,005.679688 USDT according to Binance data, amid broader market volatility.

items.title: Decred Surges 9.9% on Governance and Staking Momentum
items.summary: Decred (DCR) rallied 9.9% to $35.34 with $16M volume, driven by strong community governance and increased staking activity boosting confidence.

items.title: LayerZero Gains 7% on Omnichain Adoption
items.summary: LayerZero (ZRO) rose 7.0% to $1.64 with $111M trading volume, fueled by growing adoption of its interoperability protocol and integration announcements.

items.title: Bitcoin Faces Further Decline to $54,420 Per Bollinger Bands
items.summary: Bollinger Bands on monthly BTC/USD chart indicate buying before $54,420 may be premature, as it nears the lower band after a 30% drop, potentially signaling a cycle bottom like in 2022.

items.title: Opinion: Crypto Winter Demands Real-Asset Stablecoin Reforms
items.summary: Bitcoin, down 48.5% from its $126,272 ATH, has lost safe-haven status; stablecoins backed by real assets are seen as crypto's future, with calls for CLARITY Act legislation.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops to $65,290 Amid Macro Pressure but Institutional Demand Returns
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to $65,290 on Feb 27, down 14% for February, facing macro pressures but showing signs of returning institutional demand.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: GGML.ai Acquired by Hugging Face
items.summary: Hugging Face has acquired GGML.ai, a key player in efficient AI model inference tools. This move strengthens Hugging Face's position in optimized machine learning deployments.

items.title: David Silver Launches Ineffable Intelligence Startup
items.summary: DeepMind's David Silver starts Ineffable Intelligence, focusing on advanced world models in AI. The startup aims to push boundaries in AI reasoning and simulation.

items.title: Meta Signs Multi-Billion Deal to Rent Google AI Chips
items.summary: Meta has entered a multi-year, multi-billion-dollar agreement to rent Google's AI chips amid surging demand for compute resources. This highlights intensifying competition in AI infrastructure.

items.title: India Announces Three Domestically-Built AI Models
items.summary: India launches three new domestically developed AI models to boost local innovation. This development supports national efforts in AI self-reliance.

items.title: VoiceBox Released as Open-Source Local AI Voice Cloning
items.summary: VoiceBox, a new open-source tool for local-first AI voice cloning, is now available. It enables high-quality voice synthesis without cloud dependency.

items.title: Taalas Chips Achieve 17,000 Tokens/Second Inference
items.summary: Taalas unveils chips capable of running AI models at 17,000 tokens per second without CPU or GPU reliance. This breakthrough promises ultra-fast inference for edge applications.

items.title: Claude Coding in COBOL Triggers IBM Shares Crash
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude AI coding in COBOL reportedly caused a sharp drop in IBM shares, wiping out $31 billion in market value. The incident underscores AI's market impact.

items.title: Basis Raises $100M Series B at $1.15B Valuation
items.summary: Agentic AI accounting platform Basis secures $100 million in Series B funding at a $1.15 billion valuation. The round fuels expansion in AI-driven financial automation.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokémon Presents February 2026 Reveals New Games Pokémon Winds and Waves for Switch 2 in 2027
items.summary: The Pokémon Presents showcased two new Game Freak-developed games, Pokémon Winds and Pokémon Waves, exclusive to Nintendo Switch 2 launching in 2027 with new starters Browt, Pombon, and Gecqua. Additional announcements include Pokémon Champions releasing April 2026 on Switch with mobile later, Pokémon Pokopia on March 5, and Pokémon XD: Gales of Darkness on Switch Online in March.[1][3][5]

items.title: Pokémon Champions Battle Game Gets April 2026 Switch Release and Mobile Version Later This Year
items.summary: A new competitive battle-focused Pokémon Champions launches on Nintendo Switch in April 2026, with a mobile version planned for later in 2026. The game emphasizes trainer battles and was highlighted in the Pokémon Presents.[1][3]

items.title: Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour Event Starts Today February 28 at 3:30 PM ET
items.summary: Epic Games announced the final Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour events on February 28 and March 1, 2026, featuring generous loot, four Legendary POIs per match, and Valentine's-themed rewards like Candy Hearts. This concludes the month-long Valentine's celebration with unique storm circles and starting weapons.[2]

items.title: Pokémon XD: Gales of Darkness Coming to Nintendo Switch Online GameCube Library in March 2026
items.summary: The GameCube spin-off Pokémon XD: Gales of Darkness joins Nintendo Switch Online's GameCube selection in March 2026, making it accessible without needing rare physical copies. This was confirmed during the Pokémon Presents alongside other updates.[1][3]

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Life Sim Launches March 5 on Switch 2 with DJ Rotom and Chef Greedent Features
items.summary: Upcoming cozy Pokémon sim Pokémon Pokopia arrives March 5 on Switch 2, featuring DJ Rotom for playing Pokémon tracks, Chef Dente Greedent for cooking, and four-player multiplayer town visits.[1][3]

items.title: Pokémon World Championships 2026 Set for San Francisco Alongside Pokémon XP
items.summary: The Pokémon World Championships 2026 will take place in San Francisco with Pokémon XP, as announced by Chris Brown of Global Esports & Events during the Pokémon Presents.[8]

items.title: Renegade Game Studios Announces EXODUS RPG and Board Game Expansions at Renegade Con
items.summary: Renegade Con revealed the EXODUS Traveler’s Handbook tabletop RPG set in the sci-fi universe of the upcoming video game, plus deluxe components for Diplomacy, Ikusa, and Axis & Allies including zombie expansions.[4]

items.title: Pokémon Trading Card Game 30th Anniversary Set and Game Boy Jukebox Announced
items.summary: A new Pokémon TCG set celebrating the 30th anniversary releases in 2026 with classic and Tag Team cards; plus the Game Boy Jukebox accessory with 45 music cartridges from original games.[3][5]

items.title: Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen Remakes Now Available on Nintendo Switch
items.summary: The Game Boy Advance remakes Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen launched today on Nintendo Switch with Pokémon Home integration.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
